comment: He's a bit performative in a way and seems to value public perception more than giving genuine advice and ensuring his students actually understand assignments and can make tangible progress

Professor Wang tries to be funny relatable and entertaining which he can be entertaining sometimes, but my main issue with him is his passive aggressive tone of voice at times. Lastly, I think it'd just be nicer if he could just be straight to the point

professor matt wang is not very effective when it comes to providing help. When I brought up implementation issues to office hours, he rarely examined the actual code or walked through the logic with me. Instead of identifying where recursion unwinds, or why method violates a spec requirement, he just asked me to go over it myself
He is knowledgeable, but I believe he could improve by giving more concise, direct guidance and being more aware of the tone he uses with students.

In my experience, Professor Matt Wang is very polished in how he speaks, which at first gives the impression of professionalism. However, after attending several office hours, I often left feeling like I did not receive the help I actually needed. His explanations tend to be lengthy which uses up a lot of time without clear or actionable advice.
Took him Spring quarter he is passive aggressive and unclear about expectations

Wang's lectures are so exciting, he's definitely one of my favorite professors I've had at UW! His lecture style is very relaxed and hilarious, and his ability to explain core cs concepts is absolutely unparalleled.

Young, talented, and enthusiastic prof. Often answer questions himself on Ed (where most CS professors let TAs do it). Never forgot to repeat questions asked by students in class for people watching the recordings. Course well structured. He and the TAs made this course really easy to follow. Grading was also very lenient compared to 122 and 123.

An amazing professor! He has managed to make coding okay, if not mildly enjoyable at times. I struggled with coding a lot and spent about 10+ hours a week doing projects for this class but it wasn't horrible. Matt is a funny guy who is caring and super supportive of all his students.

World class professor and person. Very caring dude who teaches very well, makes lectures entertaining, and just keeps it 100 in general. I felt like I really was getting what I paid for with his lectures. As a non-major, I truly gained interest in CS both because of Matt's welcoming and engaging presence and because of the excellent 121 curriculum.

Very nice professor that is very interactive with the class. Teaches the subject perfectly and makes you excited about class. Even if I wasn't a CS major I would want to take CSE 121 again just for Matt.
I loved Professor Wang's class! He made me so excited to work hard on the pretty difficult projects throughout the quarter.

Very approachable guy! He is always available for questions and replies to questions quickly on Ed Discussion. Approaches feedback as constructive criticism, 100% allows you to succeed in and outside the classroom. Only concern is that the practice quizzes are less "in-depth" than the actual quiz.

Matt Wang is an incredibly talented dude who makes CSE 121 lectures worth going to. Even if you aren't interested in CS or CE at all I would take 121 just for fun! He's funny but like in a millenial type of funny.

One of the best professors I have ever had. Matt's lectures are clear and relevant and he makes an effort to add humor to the class. He is so passionate about computer science and goes out of his way to help out students. There are lots of opportunities to improve your grade in the class, and he genuinely cares about students succeeding.
Very clear lectures and he's really silly and funny, makes cs very approachable
Great Great teacher! Loved his lectures and he makes coding so much fun. Amazing experience would take again.

One of the best professors I have ever had at UW. Caring towards students and genuinely hopes everyone in his class succeeds. His lectures are extremely clear, with the class layout being very well organized and knowing exactly when assignments/projects/forms are released, due, and when feedback is released. Super engaging, funny, and kind.
Incredible communicator. A workshop in clear, engaging communication.
Professor Wang is super organized, funny, and caring. He goes above and beyond every lecture and outside of class to make sure that his students understand the material.
Very fun professor. Class was easy and forgiving.
CSE 391; but whaever; whatever class he teaches, take it. He is a good communicator and effectively reduce the amount of time you need for learning new tricks.
Matt Wang was an amazing professor and was the reason I would wake up in the morning and go to lecture.

I had both cse 121 and cse 190e with Matt. He is so helpful in office hours and you can really tell that he cares about his students. He explains things so well and I was always looking forward to his lectures. If you're looking to take 12x courses take them with Matt!! I also highly recommend his faculty cohorts for cse majors!!

You can genuinely tell that Matt really cares about his students and classes, I've taken CSE 121 before but my previous teacher was so bad I had to retake it. Matt cleared up every confusion I had from my previous teacher. He's really good and clear at explaining concepts and will repeat questions students ask for the whole class.

He is a excellent CS prof at UW. The lectures were amazing and very engaging to listen to. Test were somewhat difficult but if you understand the material then you will be able to pass this class with at least a 3.5 and above.

He's an amazing lecturer and clearly explains all concepts. Go to his OH, even if you don't have any class-related questions because the stuff I learned was super helpful in terms of a CS path. He's super nice and understanding. In terms of the class, as long as you start assignments early, ask questions, and go to section, you should be fine.

Matt is a very good professor in terms of delivering course content in an intuitive way while also being entertaining. In terms of professor quality, I think he's among the best you can get. Regarding the course content, if you've never taken a CS course, lectures and quiz sections are vital for understanding it better.

Best lecturer! Clarify everything and really cares if students understand the content. Besides, he himself is also super kind and smart :D Talking about computer science, math or anything with him during OH is enjoyable.

THE ACTUAL GOAT. This man taught my 1st ever CS class and had me wishing I was a CS major. He is hilarious, clearly cares about his students and their grasp of the content, and is super helpful outside of class. Prof shouldn't make a huge difference bc UW designs the syllabus, but there was a Matt-sized hole in my heart for all of CSE 122. 1M/10

Great professor! Very fun and enthusiastic. The course was designed very well and TA's were always available and super helpful. It is a challenging and rewarding class taught by an amazing professor and his team of TAs. Get ready to put in work if you want to succeed!
Take Matt. He's so enthusiastic, caring, and a clear, coherent speaker. He made CSE my favorite class this quarter. Take Matt.
